block.title: Request Destination Fields
block.text: The add-on expects fields that are designated as the destination (i.e. 'dest' or similar). These should be entered in comma separated format.

block.title: Set how far back in time you want to save data
block.text: You may wish to prune older data to limit the size of the data store. In which case, here you define how long you would like save data. The format should be saved as a Splunk time modifier for search. For example, if you wish to save data for a week, you would enter -7d@d. Leaving this blank will disable timestamp pruning.

block.title: Umbrella Top 1 Million Limit
block.text: If you would like to exclude popular domains from being enriched, you can enter a number here from 1 to 1 million. Doing so will exclude the corresponding top domains in the Umbrella Top 1 Million. For example, if you enter 200, the Add On will not enrich the top 200 domains in the list. Otherwise, leave it blank.

block.title: Custom Exclusion List
block.text: If you would like to specifically exclude certain IOCs from ever being enriched, enter them in comma-separated format below. They can be domains, IPs, or file hashes. If a domain, all subdomains will also be excluded from enrichment. Otherwise, leave it blank.

block.title: Set how much data you want to save
block.text: You may wish to prune data not to exceed a specific number of rows. Set the max number of rows here. Anything excess will be deleted in time-ascending order (i.e. oldest first). Leaving this blank will disable size pruning.

block.title: Proxies
block.text: Set the IP and port of your proxy server. If this is blank, the add-on will make direct requests to the investigate api. Make sure to use the following format: ip:port. Only IP and port are required, not protocol.

block.title: Host name
block.text: Set the Splunk management server's host name, if different from this host
